title: Zoro Makes His Vow to Luffy
description: On pages 16–19, Zoro confronts Dracule Mihawk, forcing his dream of becoming the world’s greatest swordsman into a brutal and public test.
scene: The Baratie battle pauses around a duel that dwarfs the immediate conflict: Zoro faces the gap between ambition and the world’s strongest swordsman.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I'd like to see you two again,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Did you come all the way out here for my head?

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: The head of the infamous ruler of East Blue, "Don Krieg"!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: But I've already enjoyed myself here. And so I'll return back to my nap.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: No need to be so Curt.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: You may have had your fun, but I sure haven't!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: W-Why's he purposely trying to prevent that man from leaving...!?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: How about you die before leaving!?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Zoro and I'll definitely bring back Nami!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: So you make sure to get us a cook!

summary: This part is a defining moment for Zoro, showing that his dream is real enough to risk death and that his loyalty to Luffy survives defeat.
